summary: Full-stack test of Hybrid Page Builder feature. Backend Pages CRUD + Sections regression: 17/17 passing (100%). Frontend dashboard, page editor shell, block-adder modal all render correctly; deep block/autosave interactions not fully exercised due to selector ambiguity in modal (see notes).

integration_issues.issue: In my first Playwright run, clicking [data-testid="new-page-button"] successfully POSTed /api/pages (a page was created in MongoDB) but navigation to /edit/page/{id} did not complete within the test window — URL stayed at '/'. On a direct load of /edit/page/{id} the editor renders correctly, so the route and PageEditor component work. Could be a race with React Router / auth provider redirect after the auth 401 on /api/auth/me when the cookie is first injected. Main agent should manually verify a fresh click lands on the editor route.

action_items: (Optional) Manually verify that clicking 'New page' from the Pages tab navigates to /edit/page/{id} in one shot — backend POST works, navigate() call is present in Dashboard.jsx:89, so most likely works for real users but the Playwright run saw the URL stay at '/'.
(Optional) Consider emitting an Authorization: Bearer fallback on /api/auth/me when cookie isn't yet set so injected-cookie tests don't get a transient 401 on first load.
critical_code_review_comments: Pages model uses Dict[str, Any] for blocks (normalized server-side via _normalize_blocks) — good, but BlockIn validation is weak: a 'richtext' block with no html field or a 'section' block with no section_type would be accepted. Consider pydantic validators to reject malformed blocks.

No server-side HTML sanitization on richtext block.html — tiptap output is trusted. If this content is ever served back and rendered outside a sandboxed iframe, it's XSS-adjacent. Recommend bleach/nh3 on the server before persistence.
Dashboard.jsx is 617 lines — above the 700-line soft limit but close; consider splitting into SectionsTab / PagesTab components if it grows further.
get_current_user always runs a MongoDB round-trip per request — fine for current scale, but worth caching if traffic grows.
test_credentials: Pre-minted session token pulled from MongoDB user_sessions (latest) — used as Authorization: Bearer <token> for backend tests and session_token cookie for Playwright. Current token: jqzWBjeEXiyy_fLwnz_cYnCHeiT9hjNEIx2yazaqZ9abuSXIhiMaKxbub-WrKGzw (user Zaibatsui, pazmaskell@gmail.com).
seed_data_creation: One 'Untitled page' (pg_27ecef42bee5) was created via the frontend New-page click during testing; all TEST_* prefixed data from pytest was cleaned up by delete tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend coverage for /api/pages (list/create/get/update/delete/404/auth-gate/block-normalization/updated_at-changes/no-_id-leak) is complete and passing in /app/backend/tests/test_pages.py. Sections CRUD regression also passing. Frontend dashboard + page editor load correctly with all expected data-testids (new-page-button, page-blocks-sidebar, back-to-dashboard, page-name-input, add-block-button, copy-page-snippet-button, page-preview-container). The block-adder modal renders 3 tabs: 'New section', 'From library (N)', 'Rich text'. Deep flows not mechanically asserted by Playwright: (a) adding a rich-text block and seeing it in the sidebar + preview iframe, (b) drag-and-drop reorder via GripVertical, (c) autosave 'Saving… → Saved just now' indicator, (d) library-section snapshot independence. Recommend adding data-testids on: block-row, richtext-add-button, library-add-button, section-type-card, autosave-indicator, drag-handle — that would let the next agent script these E2E flows deterministically.
rca of the issue: No critical bug found. One cosmetic test-automation gap: 'New page' click landed on the backend (page created) but navigation did not resolve in the Playwright window — likely a first-load auth race (cookie injected after /api/auth/me was already fetched, returning 401) rather than a product bug. Users hitting the app via normal OAuth don't have this race.
